summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex review findings on PR #727 in the fogodev/ars-ui repo, covering checkbox reset callback gating, E2E controlled reset defaults, compound-part SSR ARIA links for Dioxus, and adapter parity lint scope.
summary.outcome: All four Codex review threads were fixed, committed as a single commit, and pushed. The PR passed all CI lanes (Coverage, Release Verification, and all feature flag checks) and Codex was retriggered for another review pass.

repo: Dioxus component child names in VNode DynamicNode are fully qualified (e.g., `ars_dioxus::input::checkbox::Description`), not short names — suffix matching is required when scanning child component names.

The xtask `adapter-parity` lint was previously scoped to documented delivery/workflow components only; the correct behavior is to apply raw Leptos-vs-Dioxus test parity failures to every implemented component.
Leptos serializes ARIA relationships correctly through its reactive attr path on first SSR pass; Dioxus does not without an explicit pre-render child scan at the Root boundary.
The `is_controlled()` distinction for checkbox state belongs in the agnostic `ars-components` core API, not re-derived in each adapter.
Dioxus wasm browser tests use a similar harness to Leptos but were missing for Button, causing a parity gap surfaced by the restored lint.

code.finding: Root component now scans children VNode DynamicNode component names for `::Description` and `::ErrorMessage` suffixes to seed machine presence before Control serializes, fixing first-pass SSR ARIA links.
New `is_controlled()` / `api_reports_whether_checked_state_is_controlled` API added so adapters can gate native reset callbacks without re-deriving core controlled-state semantics.
Adapter parity lint restored to report failures for all components; delivery/presence checks remain scoped to documented workflow components only.
New file added to close Button's Dioxus wasm browser-test parity gap with Leptos, required to make the restored adapter-parity lint pass cleanly.

workflow: CI polling used 2-minute sleep loops with batched `gh pr checks`, reactions, and GraphQL review-thread queries; this is the established pattern for the repo's Codex review cycle.
Codex review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using thread IDs, and replies are posted via `gh api` REST before resolution.
Adding focused regression tests before changing implementation behavior confirmed the failure shape (Leptos already passing, Dioxus failing) and prevented over-engineering.
Temporary `eprintln!` instrumentation was used to discover fully-qualified Dioxus component names at runtime, then removed before committing.
Commit hooks in this repo are slow/silent but complete cleanly — the pattern is to wait rather than interrupt.

friction: Dioxus does not re-export `TemplateNode`/`DynamicNode` through its prelude, requiring direct `dioxus_core` imports for VNode scanning.
Commit hook ran silently for an extended period on multiple commits, requiring repeated polling to confirm completion.
`cargo test` only accepts one test filter argument — attempting to pass two filters in one invocation silently ran only one of them.
Restoring full adapter parity lint immediately exposed a pre-existing Button Dioxus wasm coverage gap, requiring additional work before the lint could pass.
Dioxus child component names are fully qualified at the VNode level, which was not documented and required runtime instrumentation to discover.
open_items: Dioxus SSR ARIA link fix relies on scanning VNode child component name suffixes — this is a structural workaround and should be revisited if Dioxus exposes a proper pre-render hook or sibling-context mechanism in a future version.
The Button Dioxus wasm tests added are minimal parity coverage; deeper browser-interaction tests (click, keyboard, focus) may still be missing.
Codex review pass was retriggered at the end of the transcript but the final verdict (approved/new threads) was not yet known.
